transcripts.title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Discussion essay (5)| Environmental problems | Cambridge 14

segments.text:
All right, let's write another discussion essay. 
This one is also from IELTS academic 14. As usual,

we need to understand the 
statement and instruction.

Some people say that the main environmental 
problem over time is the loss of particular

species of plants and animals. Others say that 
there are more important environmental problems.

So, it's about environmental problems and one 
specific one: the loss of species of plants and

animals, and if this is the main problem or issue 
of our time or not. We need to discuss both views,

meaning that we need to say what those 
people think and why they think like that,

and then give our opinion. So, we need 
to say they think the main problem is

the loss of plants and animals because, and 
also, there are more important environmental

problems. And then we can agree with V1 
or V2 or have a totally different view.

All right, it's time to decide. For view one, 
they think like that because all animals and

plants are necessary for our planet, and 
also they have the right to live, right?

And the second one, they're more important 
problems or issues, such as climate change and

deforestation. And now my view, I actually 
support view 2, and I agree with that.

Now it's time to write. The first thing to 
write is the paraphrase. Actually, for this one,

I've decided to add an introductory sentence. You 
don't have to; it's not a must. I just think it's

a good way to start this one. Today, environmental 
issues are a growing global concern. Among these

problems, the loss of specific species 
of plants and animals is debatable. So,

these two are my introductory sentences. Again, 
it's not a must, but I found it useful here.

And now it's time to paraphrase. Some people argue 
that it is the primary environmental problem or

error. While others contend that there are more 
pressing environmental challenges that deserve

attention. So here you can add your opinion if 
you like, but I've decided to state my opinion

in my conclusion. So, in my first paragraph, I 
will discuss SV1, and in the second one, I will

discuss VO2. In my conclusion, I'll summarize 
all the main points and will state my opinion.

For the first one, if you remember, I 
said all animals and plants are necessary,

and also they have the right to live. On 
the one hand, so I'll start with this one

because I want to discuss V1. The diversity 
of life forms on Earth is essential for the

planet's overall health, and every 
species' right to coexist on Earth

should be respected. This is actually a long 
topic sentence. Writing a more specific one,

I mean having only one point would be a better 
thing to do, but this is another way to do that.

Now it's time to explain the first one. When 
species disappear, it disrupts ecological

relationships. So what is the result of that? 
This, in turn, can have consequences for humans

because they depend on these ecosystems 
for resources such as food and clean water.

Now the second point, they have the right to 
live. Furthermore, I want to add another point,

so I use "furthermore." Each plant and 
animal deserves the chance to exist,

and it is not morally right to deprive 
them of that opportunity. Our moral duty

is to safeguard all species regardless 
of their immediate usefulness to us.

So here you could add examples to develop this 
paragraph further. This could be a good exercise

for you. You could copy and paste this one and 
add your details to improve this paragraph.

In the second one, I said climate change 
and deforestation are more important,

on the other hand, so I use this one to discuss 
the second view. Issues like climate change and

deforestation have more impacts on the 
environment. Why? I need to explain.

Climate change, for example, affects every 
corner of the globe and has consequences such

as rising sea levels, extreme weather 
events, and agricultural disruptions.

Now the second one, moreover, so I want to add 
the second one. I use "moreover." Deforestation,

as a grave and urgent issue, contributes 
to plant and animal loss and accelerates

climate change. So what is the result 
of that? These challenges have a more

direct and imminent impact on the planet's 
health and human societies, resulting in

immediate threats to our environment's 
food security and overall well-being.

Now it's time to write the conclusion. 
I start with "in conclusion," then I'll

add my opinion and summarize all 
the main points. In conclusion,

I believe there are more critical environmental 
issues than biodiversity loss. So this is my

view. I use biodiversity loss to paraphrase 
the loss of species of plants and animals.

Now it's time to summarize all the main points. 
It has undeniably significant, but other paramount

concerns like climate change and deforestation 
demand imperative attention and action. These

issues have far-reaching and immediate direct 
impacts on the environment and human societies.

All right, I use 275 words for this essay, and 
I organize it into four paragraphs. I started

my introduction with two introductory 
sentences, and then I paraphrased the

statement. In my first body paragraph, I 
discuss view one, and in the second one,

I discuss view two. And in my conclusion, I stated 
my opinion and summarized all the main points.

In this slide, you can find the points that 
gave cohesion to my writing. You could pause,

take a screenshot, and have a 
read. And in the next three slides,

you will find the words and collocations from 
my essay that you could also use in yours.

All right, I hope you found it 
useful. Have a nice day. [Music]

transcripts.title: Environment; Vocabulary for Writing & Reading; Sample Essay; IELTS Writing Task 2

segments.text: Welcome to Guide2IELTS
This video is first in a series on the topic of environment
In this one, I'll explain a few terms related to the subject of environment
The aim is not to go too in depth into these concepts
but to understand them enough to be able to use them in our essays
Environment means the surroundings
Biotic refers to living organisms
whereas abiotic refers to the non-living things
Flora and fauna refer to the plants and animals respectively
I used to get confused between the words flora and fauna
Well, my trick to avoiding this confusion is
that flora sounds similar to flower
and so it refers to plants
Ecosystem or ecological system
refers to a geographical area
When we use the word ecosystem,
we mean both the living, that is, biotic
and abiotic, that is, non-living factors of an area
Biotic includes the flora and fauna of the region
whereas non-living or abiotic
includes soil, water,
air, water, the landscape, that is,
visible features of an area of land
including hills, rivers, and buildings
and even weather such as weather and humidity
Ecosystems can be very varied
They can be very big, for example, a desert
or very small, for example, a tree
This is why the word ecosystem is used in a variety of ways
The major parts of an ecosystem are water, water temperature,
plants, animals, air,
light and soil
they all work together. So,
all parts of an ecosystem are interconnected and interdependent
Any change in one affects/impacts the others
This is where the term ecological balance comes from
Ecological balance is there when all parts of an ecosystem
co-exist in harmony and
there is an equilibrium among all the biotic and abiotic elements of the ecosystem
So, ecological imbalance occurs when
this equilibrium is disturbed
This can be because of natural reasons too
but usually when we are writing or speaking about it
we focus on the human actions that
cause this imbalance
Another term that is quite the buzzword
when we talk about environmental issues is
sustainable development
Sustainable describes the controlled use of
natural resources at a steady level
so that it does not cause damage to the environment
Sustainable development is a development that meets the needs of the present
without compromising the ability of future generations
to meet their own needs
That is, development is sustainable
when we use the resources in a controlled manner
we do not overuse or misuse them
and when possible, try to replenish them
so as to preserve the quality of environment
for the future generations
The word sustainable can be used to refer to various activities
that do not harm the environment
and replenish any resources that
are used
For example, sustainable energy,
sustainable economic growth,
sustainable tourism, housing,
cities, agriculture, and business practices
An example sentence:
Cycling is an environmentally sustainable form of transport
Let's now look at a couple of terms that are not directly related to environment
but which can be used when writing or speaking about environment
because they cause environmental degradation
First, material things
physical possessions, things that you can purchase
are material things
Everything from a pin to a plane is
a material possession
What would be excluded are the relationships.
freedom, peace, and much more
things that cannot be bought
This reminds me of something I first read ages ago
but which keeps making more sense with passing time
It's here on your screen
Money can buy a house but not a home
Money can buy a bed but not sleep
and so on
So, everything money can buy is a material thing
Consumer culture is a way of living, true for most of us in the present times
where spending on material things and using them to attain a certain status in society
is a key part of one's lifestyle
A similar word is consumerism
which again refers to the idea that purchasing more products
is a desirable and positive goal
Materialistic mindset or materialism is a tendency to
consider material possessions and
physical comforts as more important than spiritual values
The opposite of this is minimalism or minimalistic lifestyle
And as the word minimal conveys
it is about owning the minimum possible material things
By the way, the adjective minimalist
does not just refer to lifestyle
My personal favourite is minimalist art
Just google it and enjoy
Let's now work on an IELTS essay question
The question is: Some think that the current generation
should takes steps to protect the environment for
the next generation
To what extent do you agree or disagree
We'll first think about what points we can write
in favour and against
This is one of those questions where
according to me at least
there are no possible points to disagree
However, what I will write is that
those who disagree may disagree becauset
they think whatever issues there are in the environment
have not been caused by us or the present generation only
so, why should we be the ones responsible for correcting it
but what I'll write in favour will be stronger points
because that's what I genuinely believe
In that, I'll talk about that
there are major environmental issues we're facing right now
climate change, etc. and
this is the time when we really need to
work on trying to stop the problems
and if possible, reverse them.
Second is that we have more knowledge, more scientific knowledge
than the previous generations to understand
what damage has been caused to the environment
and is still being caused to the environment
so, we should change it. I'll
also write about consumer culture, materialism
and how this is harming the environment
and how this is also leading to discontentment
The writing format that we follow is similar to the typical one that we do
That is, in the first paragraph
we'll write an introductory statement
followed by paraphrasing of the question
The second and third paragraphs will talk about
the two opposing viewpoints
and finally, in conclusion,
express my own viewpoint
As I keep repeating in my videos,
any formats that we follow are just to help us write a well-structured essay
and that it is not the only correct format
Usually what I do in my essays is I write about
whichever side I have more points on first
and the other one next
That is, the second paragraph is about
the side I am favouring
and the third one is about
the side I am against
In this one, I'm changing it a little
I'll write first about why people think that
it is not the responsibility of the present generation
to preserve and protect the environment
I'm choosing to do that because
I hardly have any points for that
So, I'll just mention that in one sentence and
then, in the next paragraph
focus on why we should work on
protecting the environment
Starting with the first paragraph. First,
the general statement
In today’s world, environmental degradation
is an issue that impacts every organism
on planet earth and is
one of the biggest challenges being faced by humans.
The paraphrasing now
While it is generally opined that it is our responsibility
to ensure that the environment is preserved
and protected for the future generations,
there are a few who argue against
this opinion.
The second paragraph will now talk about
what arguments are given
to support that it is not the responsibility
of the present generation
to protect the environment
Those who oppose it being the responsibility of present generation
to ensure environmental protection
assert that the existing predicament is
a result of many generations playing fast and loose
with the environment and so, why should one generation
be weighed down with this mammoth task.
Next paragraph about why think the
present generation should take care of the environment
However, taking this stand
that is, referring to the stand in the second paragraph,
reeks of shirking responsibility.
The fact remains that we have inherited a planet
which is battling perilous environmental issues
such as climate change.
As many studies have shown,
this is high time that steps are taken to
first, stop the degradation
and then, reverse it too.
Next point: The grave responsibility
falls on the humans inhabiting the earth today
as they have the scientific knowledge
that their antecedents
antecedents means anything that came before
so, the previous generation
so, they have the scientific knowledge
that their antecedents did not have
to understand the gravity of the current environmental situation
and fight it
Next reason, talking about materialism and consumerism
Moreover, one of the reasons for the ruin of environment
is the consumer culture and materialistic mentality
that have become an integral part of modern lifestyle.
These, along with harming the environment
have also resulted in
stress and dissatisfaction for people
Hence, reversing this way of living will serve the dual purpose
of ensuring ecological balance
and individual happiness
On to the conclusion now where
I'll talk about why we, the humans
need to conserve the environment not just for the future human generations
but also for all the other organisms
I strongly believe that not only should we conserve the environment
for future humans but we should also
do so for all the flora and fauna of the world
The planet earth is ours for the duration we are on it
both to enjoy it and to safeguard it
therefore, sustainable development needs to be the motto of one and all
Notice the phrases in bold
understand them and try to incorporate them in your writings
If you face any issues, just drop a message
and we'll try to explain them in the future videos
I'll leave you with IELTS Writing task 2 questions
which can be answered
using the concepts discussed in this video
Some people think that resources should be spent on protecting wild animals
while others think those would be better used
for the human population.
Discuss both sides and give your opinion
This question can be answered by citing the ecological balance and its importance and
how it benefits every element
biotic and abiotic
including humans
of the earth's ecosystems
The second question
Some think that climate change reforms
will negatively affect business
Others feel they are an opportunity
for businesses.
Discuss both views and give your own opinion
The essay written in response to this one
can talk about corporate social responsibility
carbon footprint and
green and sustainable development
and how these can be a
boon for businesses and
how these are the socially responsible things to do
We'll cover other topics related to environmental issues
such as pollution and other related terms
such as carbon footprint and
corporate social responsibility
in our next video
Do like and share this video if you found it helpful
and subscribe to our channel
We look forward to your comments and feedback
Bye for now. All the very best.

transcripts.title: how to describe a life cycle diagram in ielts writing task 1

segments.text:
What’s up everyone. Welcome to my channel. 
Today, I’ll use the salmon as an example

to show you how to describe a life cycle.
Let’s start with the overview paragraph.

It can be your second paragraph 
or your last paragraph.

But I personally prefer my second 
paragraph to be my overview paragraph.

I know a lot of IELTS gurus say 
oh, process diagrams are easy.

In the overview, you just say the life cycle is a 
natural process that begins with…and ends with….

Let me explain why this is such 
a bad overview of a life cycle.

If we use this method to summarize the 
life cycle of the salmon, we’ll have:

The life cycle of the salmon is a 
natural process that begins with

the laying of eggs and ends with adult 
salmon returning to their birthplace.

First of all, it’s not idiomatic at all 
to say a life cycle is a natural process.

I mean, is there any life cycle 
that isn’t a natural process?

Would you call the life cycle of 
a human being a natural process?

You wouldn't, would you?
There is just no need to mention “natural”.

It’s so obvious.
If it’s a life cycle, then it’s a natural process.

Secondly, it’s pointless to say that 
a life cycle begins with the laying of

eggs and ends with the adult stage because 
almost all life cycles are like that.
Take for example the life cycle of the silkworm.

Doesn’t it also begin with the egg 
stage and end with the adult stage?

That is to say, you won’t make a life cycle 
distinctive from other lifecycles if you talk

about only the first and last stages of the cycle.
I think, in the overview paragraph,

it would be much better to include 
all the stages of a cycle.

It’s pretty clear that there are four stages in 
the life cycle of the salmon: the egg stage, the

fry stage, the smolt stage, and the adult stage.
So, we can say: Overall, salmon have an average

lifespan of 9 years and their life cycle consists 
of four main stages: egg, fry, smolt, and adult.

Here, “egg” means the egg stage, “fry” means 
the fry stage, “smolt” means the smolt stage

and “adult” means the adult stage.
But we can omit “the” and “stage”.

Also note the question uses “the 
salmon”. However, I use “salmon”.

“The salmon” refers to the 
species so it’s singular.

It requires a singular verb.
The salmon has an average lifespan of 9 years.

“Salmon”, on the other hand, refers 
to the fish themselves so it’s plural.

And please keep in mind that the plural form 
of salmon is also "salmon", not “salmons”.

Salmon have an average lifespan of 9 years.
“Salmon” is plural here, so I use

the plural verb “have”.
Both versions are correct.

But, here, “salmon” is actually better than “the salmon”.
This is because you need more than

one to get an average.
"The salmon has an average"

doesn't sound as good because the species doesn't 
have a lifespan, and one salmon doesn't have an

average life span but a group of salmon do.
Normally, this is all you need to write

for an overview of a life cycle.
But here I will also make a comparison.

The first three stages happen in a rive, 
or you can say They happen in freshwater.

The last stage, however, happens in the 
sea, or you can say in saline ˈseɪlin water.

While the first three stages occur in freshwater, 
the last one takes place in saline water.

This is the entire overview paragraph.
Now, let’s move on to Details Paragraph 1.

I would group the first three stages into this 
paragraph since they all happen in freshwater.

So, the life cycle starts 
when salmon eggs are laid.

I think the hard part is to 
describe where the eggs are laid.

There is a lot of information here.
I would start from the stones.
The eggs are laid among stones.

So we can say: The life cycle starts when 
salmon eggs are laid among small stones.

Then we need to modify the stones.
Be careful with where the stones actually are.

Don’t say something like "the stones 
are covered by reeds" or "the stones are

under reeds" because they sound like the reeds 
have been cut and laid across the stones.

To make it clear that the reeds are growing out 
of the stony area, we can say something like

"among small stones at the base of reeds" or 
"among small stones interspersed among reeds".

Intersperse means scatter 
among or between other things.

Both versions are good but I don’t like to 
use “big” words, so I will use this version.

Now we need to modify the reeds.
Where are the reeds?

They’re in the upper river.
So we can say:
at the base of reeds in the upper part of a river

Now we need to modify the river and add the 
information “slow moving”: in the upper part

of a river, where the current is slow.
So, the egg stage is done.

Now, let’s move on to the fry stage.
This diagram is actually very misleading.

It doesn’t take 5 to 6 months 
for salmon eggs to hatch.

It takes only 2 to 3 months.
And after the eggs hatch,

what come out of the eggs are not fry but alevins.
The alevins hide among the stones for 3-4

months to develop into fry.
That’s 5-6 months in total.

Of course, we don’t need to talk 
about the alevins in our essay.

But still, we should know that there is a stage 
missing so that we won’t write something stupid

like: After 5-6 months, the eggs hatch.
Or, after the eggs hatch, fry emerge.

Again, it takes only 2-3 
months for the eggs to hatch.

And after the eggs hatch, what emerge are not fry.
You know how tiny salmon eggs are.

There is no way something that’s 3-8 cm 
long can come out of a tiny salmon egg.

So, how can we handle this part?
We can simply say:

After approximately 5 to 6 months, 
fry can be seen in the water.
This is enough.

And there’re a couple of things 
I want to talk about here.

Firstly, I wanna talk about the verb “emerge”.
In the context of a life cycle,

it means "come out of ".
After the eggs hatch, alevins emerge.

You should use this verb only when you describe 
something that comes out of something like an egg.

So, it is incorrect to say fry emerge.
Fry don’t emerge.

They don’t come out of eggs.
Alevins do.

Even though here we don’t have an 
opportunity to use the verb “emerge”,

better learn it because we may need to 
use it when describing other life cycles.

Take for example the life cycle 
of the silkworm, you can say

after the eggs hatch, larvae emerge.
This means larvae come out of the eggs.

And you can also use “emerge” in this stage.
Moths emerge from the cocoons, which means

moths come out of the cocoons.
The other thing I want to talk about

is that the plural form of “fry” is also “fry”.
So, we say fry can be seen in the water,

not “fries” can be seen in the water.
Now let’s go back to the fry stage.

Let’s add the information “3-8 cm”.
I will show you four different ways to do it.

Use the verb “measure”.
They measure 3 to

8 centimeters long.
Use the verb “reach”.

They reach 3 to 8 centimeters long.
Use the verb “average”.

They average 3 to 8 centimeters long.
And of course, you can simply say

They are 3 to 8 centimeters long.
I’m going to use “measure” here.

Fry which measure 3 to 8 centimeters 
long can be seen in the water.

Now let’s add the information 
“lower river” and “fast flowing”.

The fry then swim to the lower, 
faster-flowing part of the river.

So they stay here for about 4 years and 
then they become smolts and the smolts

are about 12-15 cm long.
Let’s organize these three

pieces of information into one sentence.
After growing and feeding here for about 4 years,

they reach 12 to 15 centimeters 
long and are considered smolts.

As I showed you earlier in this video, here we 
can use the verb "reach", "measure", or "average".

Also note the use of “ be considered”.
So we want to say they reach 12 cm long and

become smolts.
But “considered”

is more formal and therefore better.
Now, let’s move on to the final adult stage.

So, the smolts swim to the ocean, and 
there they develop into adult salmon.

You can say they develop into adult salmon, or grow 
into adult salmon, or mature into adult salmon.

They then head to the ocean where 
they mature into adult salmon

Now let’s add their body size: which 
average 70 to 76 centimeters long.

Finally, let’s finish the life cycle.
They spend around 5 years there, after which they

migrate upstream to their birthplace to spawn, 
and the entire journey is repeated all over again.

Please note here “which” doesn’t refer 
to “5 years” but refers to the whole

“spending five years there”.
That’s why “which” is used.

Or you can say: They spend around 
5 years there. Period. After that,

they migrate to their birthplace.
So far we’ve finished the overview paragraph,

Details Paragraph 1, and Details paragraph 2.
Now, let's finish the introductory paragraph.

In this paragraph, we paraphrase 
the introductory text.

The diagrams below show the life cycle of 
a species of large fish called the salmon.

You seriously shouldn’t change 
any of the underlined terms.
Let’s try to use different grammar.

The diagrams illustrate the 
life cycle of the salmon

And then we can use a which-clause to modify 
the salmon: which is a large fish species.

This is the entire essay.
Let’s recap what we’ve learned.

When describing a life cycle, in the overview, 
we should state all the main stages in the cycle.

We can use “the life cycle begins” 
to start our details paragraphs.

And use “the entire journey is repeated all 
over again” to end our details paragraphs.

And here’re some useful verbs you can use 
to describe the transformation of an animal.

Alevins emerge from the eggs, which 
means they come out of the eggs.

The fry mature into, or develop into, 
or grow into, or transform into smolts.
Or use the verb “consider”.

They are considered smolts.
That’s all for this video.

And here is another video in which I talk 
about how to describe a manufacturing cycle.

It’s actually very different 
from describing a life cycle.

Be sure to check it out.
And if you found this video helpful,

please give it a thumbs-up, and don’t forget to 
subscribe to my channel. Thanks for watching. Bye.
